findings.summary: docs/guidance-lifecycle.md and ai_router/CHANGELOG.md documented guidance_report as stamping in-file headers without noting the required --write-headers flag (the bare command is read-only).
findings.location: docs/guidance-lifecycle.md (Cost Reporter section); ai_router/CHANGELOG.md (0.19.0 reporter bullet)
findings.resolution: Lifecycle doc now shows three invocations (bare=read-only report, --write-headers=stamp, --check=gate), states read-only-by-default, and that only --write-headers mutates; commands table notes the flags. CHANGELOG reporter bullet rewritten to read-only-by-default + --write-headers stamps + --check gates. Confirmed resolved R2.

findings.summary: The scaffolded project-guidance.md starter told consumers to read docs/planning/session-set-authoring-guide.md, a file the D7 scaffold does not create (so a fresh consumer repo is pointed at a nonexistent local file).
findings.resolution: Bullet now reads 'read this file and docs/planning/lessons-learned.md' (both scaffolded) and references the authoring guide via a canonical GitHub URL, noting a fresh repo has no local copy. Golden fixtures regenerated; TS suite green. Confirmed resolved R2.

findings.summary: The disuse-archival rule ('When to archive') and the rare-but-critical example ('Promotion is orthogonal to archival') were worded inconsistently — one implied disuse candidacy, the other said rare-but-critical lessons are 'not a candidate'.
findings.resolution: R1 fix added an operator-review exemption clause to the Disused bullet; R2 flagged a residual phrasing mismatch; R2 fix harmonized BOTH bullets to: a rare-but-critical lesson may cross the disuse window and surface as a candidate by the raw rule, but is spared at operator review and not archived for disuse. Confirmed resolved R3 (VERIFIED).
